examples: I listened to his words very closely

meanings.definition: the text of a popular song or musical-comedy number
examples: his compositions always started with the lyrics
he wrote both words and music
the song uses colloquial language

meanings.definition: language that is spoken or written
examples: he has a gift for words
she put her thoughts into words
they had a quarrel
they had words

meanings.definition: words making up the dialogue of a play
examples: the actor forgot his speech

meanings.definition: a unit of language that native speakers can identify
examples: words are the blocks from which sentences are made
he hardly said ten words all morning
he didn't say a word about it

meanings.definition: information about recent and important events
examples: they awaited news of the outcome
when I give the word, charge!

meanings.definition: an exchange of views on some topic
examples: we had a good discussion
we had a word or two about it
he gave his word

meanings.definition: a word is a string of bits stored in computer memory
examples: large computers use words up to 64 bits long

meanings.definition: the divine word of God; the second person in the Trinity (incarnate in Jesus)
a secret word or phrase known only to a restricted group
examples: he forgot the password

meanings.definition: the sacred writings of the Christian religions
examples: he went to carry the Word to the heathen

meanings.definition: put into words or an expression
examples: He formulated his concerns to the board of trustees
